A crimson dragon unleashes torrents of fire over a granite fortress, stone walls melting like wax as towers collapse inward, soldiers scattering like ash across burning battlements.
A bronze dragon swoops across a valley battlefield, its roar scattering ten thousand soldiers as horses stampede and shield walls shatter beneath the hurricane of its wingbeats.
An obsidian dragon lands atop a mountain summit during a thunderstorm, folding cathedral-sized wings as lightning illuminates its silhouette against roiling black clouds and wind-torn mist.
A magma-scaled dragon erupts from a volcano's caldera, lava cascading off its wings as pyroclastic clouds and beast merge into one apocalyptic vision of fire and ash.
A golden dragon rears among its treasure hoard, surrounded by a dozen knights with raised spears, its throat glowing orange as it prepares to incinerate the would-be thieves.
Seven dragons of different colors soar in formation across autumn mountains, their shadows rolling over forests and villages like the hand of an ancient god passing judgment.
A sapphire dragon and armored rider dive through enemy cavalry, flames carving a molten path as the rider's lance pierces a warlord's banner, army collapsing into chaos.
An emerald dragon strafes a war fleet at dawn, green fire consuming sails and masts, longships breaking apart as sailors leap into churning seas beneath choking smoke.
A silver dragon sleeps coiled around mountains of gold coins and jeweled crowns in a cathedral-like cavern, its breathing making treasures glitter like a metallic tide.
Two dragons—one white, one black—spiral in aerial combat above snow-capped peaks, claws raking scales, blood raining down on glaciers as thunder echoes from their collisions.
A pale dragon circles above a coronation in a castle courtyard, its shadow passing over the kneeling king as nobles watch in terror and awe, uncertain of blessing or curse.
A frost dragon encased in a glacier's heart begins to crack the ice from within, one golden eye opening as ancient magic fails and the mountain trembles with awakening.
Three dragon eggs crack open in a firelit stone chamber, hatchlings—ruby, amber, jade—emerging wet and mewling, eyes opening to a world that will soon tremble before them.
An iron-gray dragon perches atop a cathedral's spire, wings draped over bell towers, claiming the conquered city as its territory while smoke rises from burning districts below.
A violet dragon plummets from storm clouds in a near-vertical dive, wings tucked tight against its body, targeting a fleeing column of refugees on a mountain pass.
A cobalt dragon flies through a lightning storm, each thunderbolt illuminating its serpentine form in stark white flashes as electricity dances along its wings and horns.
A copper dragon marches alongside ten thousand warriors across a desert plain, its footfalls shaking the earth as war drums match its heartbeat, an unstoppable living siege weapon.
A jade dragon coils three times around a wizard's tower, its enormous head level with the highest window, eyes glowing as it whispers ancient secrets to the sorcerer within.
A burgundy dragon lies crashed in a wheat field, dozens of arrows and ballista bolts protruding from its hide, still roaring defiance as soldiers approach with trembling spears.
A pearl-white dragon soars past a rising sun, its translucent wings glowing amber and gold, silhouetted perfectly against the molten sky above a sleeping valley.
An ice-blue dragon materializes from a howling blizzard, frost coating its crystalline scales as it descends on a snow-buried fortress, breath freezing soldiers mid-scream into statues.
A navy dragon explodes from ocean depths beside a merchant vessel, seawater cascading off scales like waterfalls, jaws wide enough to swallow the ship's mainmast whole.
A colossal ribcage rises from desert sands like a cathedral of bone, the ancient dragon skeleton large enough that caravans pass between its vertebrae seeking shade.
A scarlet wizard stands on a cliff's edge, staff raised, as a midnight-black dragon lands before him, the two locked in silent communion while purple energy crackles between them.
Dozens of dragons—every color of sunset and storm—migrate across an autumn sky above a coastal kingdom, their shadows turning day to twilight as citizens watch in hushed reverence.
A moss-green dragon tears through a forest canopy, ancient trees splintering like twigs beneath its bulk as it pursues fleeing hunters, leaves and branches exploding in its wake.
An amber dragon rises from ruins of a burned library, manuscripts and scrolls clutched in its claws, the last knowledge of a fallen civilization stolen into the sky.
A charcoal dragon perches on a narrow mountain bridge, wings spread wide to block an advancing army, flames licking from its nostrils as generals realize their invasion ends here.
A bone-white dragon crashes through ice covering a frozen lake, pulling a mammoth from the depths in its jaws, water and frost erupting around its emergence.
A rose-gold dragon stands in a throne room's ruins, one claw resting on a shattered crown, smoke curling from its nostrils as the dynasty it just ended burns outside.
A teal dragon banks sharply through a canyon, wingtips nearly scraping stone walls as it pursues griffin riders, the narrow passage amplifying its thunderous roar into an earthquake.
A slate-gray dragon hovers mid-air with massive wingbeats, clutching a stone tower in its claws and tearing it from a fortress, defenders still visible in the windows.
A burgundy hatchling takes its first flight from a cliff nest, mother dragon watching as the young one's uncertain wingbeats catch wind and soar over forested valleys.
An indigo dragon breathes lightning instead of fire, bolts arcing across a battlefield to explode siege engines and cook armored knights in their plate, air crackling with ozone.
A rust-red dragon excavates its lair in a mountainside, claws tearing through solid rock like clay, boulders tumbling down to crush the village below in an avalanche.
A seafoam dragon surfaces beside a lighthouse during a hurricane, waves crashing over its barnacle-encrusted scales as it coils around the tower in an ancient territorial claim.
A golden elder dragon lies dying on a battlefield, surrounded by the broken weapons of a thousand warriors, its final breath washing over a young prince in legend-making flame.
A plum-colored dragon snatches a fleeing ship's captain from his deck, ascending into clouds as crew watches helplessly, the tyrant's screams fading into thunder above.
A lime-scaled dragon erupts through arena gates during gladiatorial games, spectators stampeding as the beast claims the coliseum, roaring triumph over scattered fighters and lions.
A storm-gray dragon and its rider land before an enemy king's tent at dawn, the warrior dismounting to issue challenge while the beast's shadow engulfs the entire encampment.
A citrine dragon melts through a castle's iron portcullis with sustained fire-breath, metal running like water as defenders scramble backward from the invading inferno beyond.
An obsidian hatchling emerges from its egg already breathing small flames, instantly incinerating the cultists who stole it, the temple becoming its first conquest and funeral pyre.
A turquoise dragon dives underwater to battle a sea serpent, the ocean's surface exploding with their struggle as ships are caught between titan and dragon in foam-whipped chaos.
A sand-colored dragon emerges from a desert dune like a breaching whale, showering a caravan with earth as it surfaces, ancient and terrible, disturbed from centuries of sleep.
A lavender dragon crashes through a cathedral's rose window during a siege, glass and lead raining on the soldiers sheltering inside as divine sanctuary becomes a dragon's maw.
An onyx dragon stands in a mountain pass between two armies, neither side willing to advance, the beast's presence transforming political war into survival's brutal calculus.
A silver-blue dragon flies parallel to an avalanche it triggered, racing the cascading snow down a mountain toward a fortress built too ambitiously on the slope below.
A saffron dragon incubates its eggs with controlled fire-breath in a volcanic cave, the devoted parent's silhouette framed by lava flows and the promise of a new dragon generation.
A midnight-blue dragon descends through morning fog onto a battlefield's aftermath, feeding on the dead, a grim reminder that dragons are nature's cleanup crew and humanity's hubris.
A vermillion dragon breaks a siege by landing atop the enemy's siege tower, the entire structure collapsing under its weight, wood and soldiers crushed as the city's salvation arrives.
A thunderstorm-gray dragon shatters a fortress gatehouse with its tail, the entire barbican exploding into rubble as the beast crawls through breached walls, soldiers crushed beneath scraping claws.
A bone-white dragon lands in a valley's center as opposing armies freeze mid-charge, its roar ending the battle before swords clash, twenty thousand warriors united in terror.
A hurricane-blue dragon alights on a coastal cliff during sunset, waves crashing hundreds of feet below, folding wings slowly as seabirds scatter from its ancient perch.
A lava-veined dragon surfaces from a fissure splitting open mid-battlefield, magma pouring around its emergence, armies fleeing as geography itself becomes the monster's entrance.
A copper-scaled dragon rears in its cliffside nest as wyverns attack, three lesser beasts against one true dragon, the mother's roar shaking loose stones down the mountainside.
Twelve dragons fly in V-formation over winter tundra, their varied colors—crimson, emerald, gold—brilliant against endless white, migration older than the kingdoms they pass over.
A storm-colored dragon and cloaked rider barrel through a shield wall, the beast's claws shredding armor while the rider's war-hammer crushes helms, breakthrough incarnate.
A deep-green dragon skims across a harbor at midnight, acid breath melting through warship hulls, entire fleet sinking in minutes as the beast banks for another pass.
An ancient brass dragon drowses atop a hoard in firelight, coins and gems cascading off its flanks with each breath, stalactites glittering in the treasure-cave's vaulted dark.
Two dragons—scarlet and frost-white—grapple mid-air above a burning forest, their combat started the blaze below, fire and ice magic bleeding from wounds across scales.
A shadow-black dragon circles a wedding ceremony in a castle garden, the bride fainting as nobles scatter, the beast's intention unreadable, orbiting like a vulture or guardian angel.
A primordial dragon frozen in a mountain's ice begins cracking free, entire cliff-face calving away as one crystalline wing breaks through, the glacier groaning with ancient awakening.
Four dragon eggs hatch simultaneously in a ruined tower, whelps—silver, bronze, obsidian, gold—crawling over each other toward firelight, chirping cries that sound like distant thunder.
A rust-colored dragon sprawls across a castle's curtain wall, tail wrapped around one tower, head resting on another, claiming the fortress like a cat claims a sunny windowsill.
A peregrine-gray dragon drops from overcast skies in a hunting stoop, wings fully tucked, diving toward a royal carriage on a forest road at terminal velocity.
A brass dragon flies through a thunderstorm's heart, lightning striking its horns and back, each bolt illuminating the creature in x-ray flashes, unharmed and exultant in the tempest.
A war-scarred bronze dragon walks beside a barbarian horde across steppes, the ground shaking with synchronized footfalls, riders raising axes in rhythm with the dragon's measured stride.
A serpentine emerald dragon wraps around a lighthouse tower five times, only its head and tail visible at top and bottom, claiming the navigation point as territorial marker.
A cardinal-red dragon lies in a meadow surrounded by ballista bolts, one wing shattered, breathing labored, yet incinerating the first three knights who approach with dying fury.
A dawn-pink dragon glides past the rising moon, backlit perfectly against its silver face, wings translucent in lunar glow above a monastery where monks chant morning prayers.
A glacier-blue dragon materializes in a whiteout blizzard, ice crystallizing along its scales, descending on fur-wrapped warriors whose torches gutter out in frozen wind.
A turquoise dragon breaches beside a pirate galley like a whale, seawater streaming from barnacled scales, jaws snapping the ship's stern clean off before submerging again.
A titan's skeleton dominates a wasteland, dragon bones so massive that its skull serves as a chieftain's hall, ribcage sheltering an entire nomadic camp beneath arching ivory.
A violet-robed sorceress faces a charcoal dragon across a stone circle, her hands glowing with binding runes while the dragon's eyes burn with rebellion against ancient pacts.
A migration of thirty dragons darkens the sky above a mountain kingdom, every color and size, their passage taking an hour, children watching in wonder as parents murmur prayers.
An amber dragon smashes through an ancient forest, pursuing a stag, each wingbeat toppling century-old oaks, the hunter carving a scar through woodland that stood before kingdoms rose.
A sand-yellow dragon rises from a burning archive, scrolls and tomes clutched protectively against its chest, carrying humanity's history into the sky as conquerors torch the library below.
A gunmetal dragon blocks a mountain pass entirely, wings spread wall-to-wall between cliffs, flames dripping from jaws as it regards the approaching army with hunger and contempt.
A pearl-white dragon shatters through a frozen river's surface, dragging an elk in its jaws, ice shards glittering in winter sun, spray freezing mid-air around its emergence.
An ash-gray dragon stands in a demolished throne room, one claw crushing the fallen crown into the marble floor, smoke billowing from nostrils as dynasty ends in rubble.
A sapphire dragon rolls mid-flight through a narrow canyon, dodging crossbow fire from both walls, scales screaming against stone as it corkscrews through the gauntlet toward fleeing archers.
A moss-green dragon hovers above a castle, clutching a torn-free tower in all four claws, soldiers falling from windows as it carries the stone cylinder skyward like prey.
A young gold dragon wobbles on its first flight from a mountain eyrie, parent watching as uncertain wingbeats find thermal updrafts, the hatchling gaining confidence over alpine lakes.
A violet dragon exhales crackling lightning across a siege line, electricity chaining between metal armor, cooking dozens in an instant as ozone stench replaces the smell of fear.
A carmine dragon excavates a new lair in a cliff face, each claw-strike shearing away tons of rock, landslide burying the trade road below under the dragon's excavated mountain.
A sea-green dragon surfaces in a typhoon, coiling around a crumbling lighthouse, scales covered in kelp and coral, reclaiming the structure during the storm that will erase human presence.
An ancient gold dragon dies surrounded by shattered armies, its last exhalation a gout of flame that anoints a farm-boy in dragon-fire, the chosen survivor of an age's ending.
A plum-scaled dragon snatches a fleeing general from his horse mid-gallop, ascending with the screaming commander as his army watches their leader carried into storm clouds and legend.
A chartreuse dragon explodes through coliseum gates during executions, spectators trampled in panic as the beast claims the arena, standing over cowering prisoners it has inadvertently freed.
A slate-blue dragon and rider dismount before a parley tent at sunrise, the warrior offering terms while the dragon looms behind, its shadow swallowing the enemy camp in threat.
A bronze dragon melts through a castle's main gate with sustained acid-breath, metal and wood dissolving like wax as defenders flee the caustic fumes preceding invasion.
A newborn crimson dragon ignites within its hatching egg, immolating the thieves who stole it from the nest, the cave becoming a funeral pyre before the hatchling's first breath.
An azure dragon dives beneath ocean waves to battle a kraken, tentacles wrapping its body as both titans thrash, surface boiling with their struggle beneath horrified sailors.
A dune-colored dragon erupts from desert sand beneath a merchant caravan, cascading earth swallowing camels and wagons as the disturbed ancient rises from centuries-long slumber below.
A mauve dragon crashes through stained glass during a cathedral siege, multicolored shards raining on praying refugees as sanctuary becomes slaughterhouse, beauty turned to horror.
A shadow-black dragon stands between two armies in a mountain pass, neither side advancing, the impasse created by a creature whose presence transcends human conflict entirely.
A platinum dragon flies alongside an avalanche it triggered, racing cascading snow toward a fortress, destruction and destroyer parallel in their descent toward the valley.
A copper-red dragon curls around its clutch in a volcanic cave, breathing measured flames to warm eggs, maternal devotion framed by lava flows and sulfurous devotion.
A navy-blue dragon lands on a dawn battlefield among the dead, feeding methodically on corpses, nature's undertaker reminding victors and vanquished that dragons outlast all kingdoms.
A flame-orange dragon shatters an enemy siege tower by landing atop it, the entire structure pancaking under tons of scale and muscle, relieving the besieged city through devastating arrival.
A bone-white dragon tears through a fortress's eastern curtain wall with its horns, the entire section collapsing into the courtyard as defenders are crushed beneath limestone blocks and dragonscale.
An ash-gray dragon lands in no-man's-land between clashing armies, its roar freezing fifty thousand warriors mid-charge, banners drooping as survival instinct overrides loyalty and orders.
A storm-black dragon perches on a windswept summit at twilight, folding wings the size of war galleys as its silhouette merges with thunderheads bruising the horizon behind it.
A sulfur-yellow dragon emerges from a volcanic crater mid-eruption, lava sheeting off its scales like water as ash clouds and wyrm rise together in apocalyptic union.
A forest-green dragon guards its nest against a dozen adventurers in a cavern glittering with plundered relics, throat igniting orange as thieves realize greed has become their funeral.
Nine dragons in pearl, crimson, jade, and bronze flight-formation cross a wheat-gold prairie at harvest time, their shadows sweeping over villages like the eclipse of an older age.
A cobalt dragon and silver-armored knight crash through pikemen ranks at full gallop, fire cutting through infantry like a blade through silk as kingdoms change hands in minutes.
A wine-dark dragon strafes a invasion fleet at anchor, purple flames consuming rigging and hulls, longboats becoming pyres as ash-choked sailors drown in harbor water.
An ancient copper dragon sleeps atop a mountain of swords, crowns, and golden chains in a natural vault, each slow breath making treasure-heaps shift like metallic dunes.
Two dragons—chalk-white and oil-black—clash above a glacier, talons locked, spiraling earthward as blood flash-freezes in the air and ice cracks beneath their inevitable impact.
A sand-colored dragon orbits a desert coronation slowly, its shadow crossing the kneeling sultan three times as priests argue whether this omen blesses or dooms the reign.
A cerulean dragon trapped in a cliff-face of blue ice opens one eye behind the frozen wall, cracks spidering outward as the glacier shudders with breath after a thousand years.
Five dragon eggs split open in a ruined chapel, hatchlings in crimson, silver, moss, saffron, and shadow crawling toward daylight filtering through the shattered roof above.
A steel-gray dragon sprawls across a cathedral roof, each limb claiming a different spire, tail wrapped around the belltower as conquered citizens emerge to witness their new master.
A plum-purple dragon drops through cloud-cover in a vertical dive, wings pressed tight against flanks, plummeting toward a royal barge on a river far below like judgment incarnate.
A brass dragon flies through a lightning storm's violence, each thunderbolt striking its horns and spine, illuminating scale patterns in strobing white as it roars challenge at the tempest.
A sand-red dragon walks beside a nomadic horde across salt flats, each footfall shaking the earth in rhythm with war-drums as ten thousand warriors chant its ancient name.
A willow-green dragon coils seven times around a wizard's tower, only its great horned head visible at the window-slit where sorcerer and drake commune in the old tongue.
A wine-red dragon lies broken in a barley field, forty crossbow bolts jutting from its hide, yet still incinerating the first rank of approaching soldiers with labored, defiant breath.
A mother-of-pearl dragon soars past the dawn sun, its translucent wings glowing rose and gold, perfectly framed against the burning sky above a fishing village waking below.
A frost-white dragon manifests from howling snow, icicles hanging from its jaw, descending on a mountain fortress as its breath turns men to frozen statues mid-scream.
An ocean-blue dragon erupts beside a treasure galleon, barnacles crusting its scales, seawater cascading off wings as jaws snap the ship's mast in half like kindling.
A dragon skeleton dominates the badlands, ribs arching cathedral-high above the desert floor where caravans shelter from sandstorms inside the hollow chest of ancient death.
A hedge-witch in raven-black robes confronts a rust-colored dragon across a standing-stone circle, her hands blazing with ward-magic while the dragon's eyes burn with contract-bound rage.
Forty dragons of every hue migrate across autumn skies above a coastal city, their passage lasting until sunset, fishermen abandoning nets to watch gods crossing overhead.
A teal dragon smashes through a pine forest canopy in pursuit of elk, each wingbeat toppling trees that stood centuries, carving a scar through wilderness older than language.
A butter-yellow dragon rises from a burning monastery, illuminated manuscripts clutched carefully in its claws, carrying the last copies of lost gospels into the sky as raiders torch the library.
A thundercloud-gray dragon fills a mountain pass completely, wings spanning cliff to cliff, blocking an invasion with its body alone as flames drip from jaws and generals despair.
A ghost-white dragon shatters upward through a frozen lake's surface, a winter wolf clamped in its jaws, ice shards catching sunlight like diamonds around the breach.
A charcoal dragon stands in a ransacked palace's great hall, one claw pressing the usurped crown into marble until gold flattens, smoke curling from nostrils as tyranny ends.
A turquoise dragon barrel-rolls through a canyon under arrow-fire, scales striking sparks off stone walls as it corkscrews past ballista positions, smoke trailing from minor wounds.
A jungle-green dragon hovers with thunderous wingbeats above a fortress, clutching a watchtower torn from its foundations, soldiers falling from arrow-slits as stone rises skyward.
A juvenile bronze dragon takes its maiden flight from a sea-cliff nest, uncertain wingbeats catching updrafts as its parent watches the fledgling discover the sky's freedom.
An amethyst dragon breathes lightning across a siege camp, electricity arcing between armor and weapons, cooking men in their plate as thunder follows a half-second behind blinding light.
A brick-red dragon excavates a new lair in a mountainside, each claw-swipe shearing away boulders that tumble onto the trade road below, burying the pass under displaced geology.
A kelp-green dragon surfaces during a monsoon, wrapping around a lighthouse in five coils, scales encrusted with coral, claiming the structure as the storm erases human trespass.
A legendary gold dragon dies on a contested battlefield, its final breath washing over a slave-boy in cleansing fire, armies watching as fate chooses the unlikeliest witness.
An ink-black dragon snatches a fleeing duchess from her carriage, ascending with the screaming noblewoman as her guards fire uselessly at the creature carrying her into legend and clouds.
A lime-scaled dragon bursts through arena gates during gladiator games, sending spectators stampeding as the beast claims the colosseum, standing over cowering fighters and confused lions.
A storm-colored dragon and its rider land before an enemy king's pavilion at daybreak, the warrior dismounting to offer terms while the dragon's shadow engulfs the entire royal camp.
An orange-red dragon melts through a fortress's portcullis with sustained firebreath, iron flowing like honey as defenders retreat from the molten barrier separating them from invasion.
A hatchling black dragon emerges from its egg already breathing flames, instantly immolating the cult that stole it, the ancient temple becoming its first conquest before first steps.
A deep-teal dragon dives beneath waves to fight a sea serpent, the ocean surface exploding with their struggle as merchant ships caught between titans capsize in foam-whipped chaos.
A dust-colored dragon breaches through desert sand beneath a spice caravan, earth cascading off its rising bulk as the disturbed ancient surfaces from its centuries-long subterranean sleep.
A lavender dragon crashes through a cathedral's rose window during a siege, multicolored glass raining on sheltering soldiers as sanctuary becomes slaughter beneath falling lead and scale.
An iron-black dragon occupies a mountain pass between two armies, neither force willing to advance past its bulk, the beast's indifferent presence ending war through simple geography.
A silver dragon races an avalanche it triggered down a mountainside, flying parallel to cascading snow toward a valley fortress built too trustingly on the slope below.
A saffron dragon curls around its egg-clutch in a volcanic hollow, breathing controlled flames to maintain temperature, maternal devotion framed by lava streams and sulfurous devotion.
A midnight-blue dragon feeds methodically on battlefield dead at dawn, nature's grim cleanup crew reminding victors and vanquished that dragons outlast all mortal kingdoms and grudges.
A vermillion dragon breaks a siege by landing atop the enemy's siege tower, the entire structure collapsing under tons of muscle and scale, crushing engineers and relieving the city.

A jade-green dragon smashes through a fortress's water gate from the river side, stone and timbers exploding inward as the beast crawls into the courtyard dripping, soldiers crushed beneath webbed claws.
A platinum dragon lands on a mesa between two frozen armies at winter dawn, its roar echoing across the snowfield so powerfully that weapons drop from numbed hands, warfare forgotten.
An ember-orange dragon alights on a volcanic peak at dusk, folding wings edged in gold as ash swirls around its form, silhouetted against the red sky like a god surveying creation.
A crystal-scaled dragon rises from a lava tube alongside a geyser of molten rock, magma streaming between its scales as mountain and monster announce themselves to the terrified valley below.
A rose-copper dragon guards its glittering hoard against a mercenary company in a sea cave, waves crashing behind the intruders as its maw glows white-hot with coming annihilation.
Fifteen dragons in emerald, ivory, crimson, and cobalt sweep across a mountain range at midday, their synchronized flight casting rippling shadows over forests that go silent beneath them.
A charcoal-gray dragon and fur-clad rider charge through a frozen shield wall, ice-breath flash-freezing warriors as the rider's axe splits helms, breakthrough made flesh and scale.
A deep-purple dragon skims a war fleet at moonrise, exhaling violet flames that consume sails and melt deck planks, warships becoming floating pyres in the harbor they meant to blockade.
A primordial brass dragon slumbers on a hoard in a dormant volcano's heart, gold coins cascading with each breath, gemstones glittering in the red glow from lava far below.
Two dragons—summer-green and winter-white—spiral in combat above a canyon, scales and blood falling like rain onto the river below as their roars shake boulders loose from cliffs.
A pale-gold dragon circles a mountain-top wedding ceremony slowly, its shadow crossing the altar three times as the bride weeps, uncertain whether blessing or curse attends her vows.
A sapphire dragon frozen in a cliff's blue ice begins fracturing free, one wing breaking through the glacier's face as entire sections calve away, the mountain groaning with ancient awakening.
Six dragon eggs crack simultaneously in a ruined watchtower, hatchlings in copper, shadow, pearl, jade, ruby, and bronze tumbling over each other toward firelight and their first sky.
A rust-orange dragon drapes itself across a conquered castle's walls, head resting on the gatehouse, tail curled around the keep, claiming the fortress like a cat claims a sunlit stone.
A slate-blue dragon plummets from overcast skies toward a royal procession on a forest road, wings tucked completely against its body in a hunting stoop that ends kingdoms.
A brass dragon flies through a summer thunderstorm, lightning striking its horns repeatedly, each bolt illuminating the creature in skeletal x-ray flashes as it roars joy at the violence.
A copper-scaled dragon marches alongside a barbarian migration across tundra, its footfalls synchronized with drums, the ground shaking as tribe and titan move as one toward conquest.
A sea-foam dragon coils around a stone lighthouse nine times from base to beacon, only head and tail-tip visible at top and bottom, marking the navigation point as eternal territory.
A burgundy dragon lies in a burned meadow pierced by dozens of spears and arrows, one wing torn, yet still cremating the first soldiers who approach with rattling, defiant breath.
A dawn-silver dragon glides across the rising sun, wings translucent and glowing like stained glass, perfectly silhouetted above a valley where shepherds kneel in involuntary reverence.
A glacier-blue dragon emerges from a whiteout blizzard, frost crystallizing along every scale, descending on a fur-wrapped war party whose torches extinguish in the frozen wind.
A deep-green dragon breaches beside a warship like a whale, kelp streaming from its barnacled hide, jaws clamping the vessel's bow and dragging it beneath the surface in seconds.
A dragon skeleton sprawls across a salt flat, bones so massive that nomads camp inside the skull, children playing between ribs that arch overhead like the pillars of a titan's temple.
A white-robed druid confronts a burgundy dragon across a moonlit stone circle, her staff blazing with nature's binding magic while the dragon's eyes burn with ancient, contract-bound fury.
Sixty dragons of every imaginable color darken the sky above a coastal kingdom in migration, their passage taking two hours, entire cities stopping to witness the flight of living gods.
A moss-green dragon crashes through an old-growth forest pursuing a wounded elk, each wingbeat toppling oaks that predate the kingdom, carving destruction through untouched wilderness.
